乐器零售商吉他中心(Guitar Center)计划在与关键投资者和贷款人谈判债务削减协议后，根据破产法第11章申请破产保护。
该公司多年来一直在为过度负债而苦苦挣扎，上周五晚些时候表示，计划继续经营下去。
这家总部位于加利福尼亚州西湖村的零售商表示，它对其整体门店面积感到满意，其中包括近300家吉他中心门店和200多家音乐与艺术门店。
但该零售商表示，它已经聘请了一家房地产公司，以探索优化其房地产投资组合的机会，并达成其他协议，专注于最有利于公司回到新冠肺炎之前的增长轨道的投资。
吉他中心出售和租赁乐器，提供课程和维修服务。该公司的其他品牌包括音乐家的朋友，木管铜管和AVDG。
像大多数非必需品零售商一样，吉他中心在疫情爆发初期被迫暂时关闭了门店。标普全球评级(S&P Global Ratings)在5月下旬的一份研究报告中写道：尽管该公司在此期间的在线收入大幅增长，但我们预计所产生的收入将不足以弥补通过门店实现的销售收入，标普全球评级公司(S&P Global Ratings)在5月下旬的一份研究报告中写道。
吉他中心周五表示，已与控股所有者达成重组协议，后者是一家由私募股权公司Ares Management管理的基金，后者在2014年收购了吉他中心的股份。该公司表示，它的交易还得到了新投资者Brigade Capital Management和凯雷集团(Carlyle Group)管理的一只基金以及多家贷款机构的支持。
吉他中心首席执行官罗恩·贾平加在一份声明中表示：“这项协议将使我们能够大幅削减债务，并对我们的业务进行再投资，以便更好地服务我们的客户，实现我们将更多音乐推向世界的使命，”吉他中心首席执行官罗恩·贾平加在一份声明中表示。
note_en:
Musical instrument retailer Guitar Center plans to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection after negotiating a debt-cutting deal with key investors and lenders.
 The company, which has been struggling with excessive debt for years, said late Friday that it plans to stay in business.
 The Westlake Village, Calif.-based retailer said it's "pleased with its overall store footprint," which includes nearly 300 Guitar Center stores and more than 200 Music & Arts stores.
 But the retailer said it had hired a real estate firm "to explore opportunities to optimize its real estate portfolio and other agreements to focus on investments that best position the Company to return to its growth trajectory prior to COVID-19."
 Guitar Center sells and rents instruments, provides lessons and handles repairs. The company's other brands include Musician’s Friend, Woodwind Brasswind and AVDG.
  Like most non-essential retailers, Guitar Center was forced to temporarily close its stores in the early days of the pandemic. While its online revenue jumped during that period, "we expect revenues generated will not cover the sales that would have occurred through the stores," S&P Global Ratings wrote in a research note in late May.
    Guitar Center said Friday it had reached a restructuring deal with its controlling owner, a fund managed by private equity firm Ares Management, which acquired its stake in Guitar Center in 2014. The company said it also got support for its deal from new investors Brigade Capital Management and a fund managed by The Carlyle Group and from various lenders.
  “This agreement will allow us to significantly reduce our debt and reinvest in our business in order to better serve our customers and deliver on our mission of putting more music in the world," Guitar Center CEO Ron Japinga said in a statement.

note:
S含有大约10%的糖。但这意味着它含有大约90%的非糖--被称为甘蔗渣(如图)的物质，一旦甘蔗被碾碎，含糖的汁液从甘蔗中榨出，这种物质就会保留下来。2017年全球蔗糖产量为1.85亿吨。这会产生大量的甘蔗渣。
目前，这其中的大部分都被烧毁了。通常，它为为磨坊提供动力的当地发电机提供燃料，因此不会被浪费。但波士顿东北大学的机械工程师朱红力(音译)认为，它可以得到更好的利用。正如她和她的同事们在本周的《物质》(Matter This Week)中所描述的那样，在咖啡杯等一次性食品容器中使用的塑料，经过一点调整后，甘蔗渣是一种极好的、可生物降解的塑料替代品。
朱博士并不是第一个有这种想法的人。但之前的尝试往往无法在与液体接触后存活下来。她认为她可以通过在甘蔗果肉中添加另一种可生物降解的材料来克服这一点。她从之前的研究中了解到，过去的努力在潮湿时失败的主要原因是甘蔗渣由短纤维组成，这些短纤维无法重叠到足以赋予成品弹性的程度。因此，她试图加入一种适当的长纤维物质。
竹子似乎符合要求。它生长迅速，容易降解，并且有适当的长纤维。而且成功了。当研究人员将少量竹浆混合到甘蔗渣中时，他们发现结果是短纤维和长纤维强烈交织在一起。令人惊喜的是，他们还发现，在这一过程中使用的热压已经动员了纤维中的一些木质素，这种坚硬的防水材料现在起到了将纤维粘合在一起的粘合剂的作用。
为了测试他们的新材料，朱博士和她的同事们首先将热油倒在上面，发现他们的发明并没有像以前的甘蔗渣产品那样渗透到材料中，相反，油被他们的发明排斥了。他们还发现，当他们用这种材料做了一个杯子，并在里面装满了加热到几乎沸点的水时，杯子保持了两个多小时的完好无损。虽然这没有塑料杯的寿命长(它可以无限期地存活)，但对于所有的实际用途来说，它已经足够长了。此外，这种新材料的强度是制造杯子的塑料的两倍，而且绝对是可生物降解的。当朱博士将一个用它制成的杯子埋在地里时，其中一半在两个月内腐烂了，她估计六个月内就会完全消失。
最后，但并非最不重要的一点是，她估计，用这种新材料制作的杯子每吨价格将达到2333美元。这是由聚乳酸(发酵植物淀粉)制成的可生物降解杯子每吨4750美元成本的一半，仅略高于制造塑料杯所需的每吨2177美元。总体而言，朱博士认为，蔗渣是制作咖啡杯、吸管、一次性盘子、轻质餐具等的明显选择。一旦使用，这些垃圾就可以问心无愧地被丢弃在垃圾填埋场。。

S  contains around 10% sugar. But that means it contains around 90% non-sugar—the material known as bagasse (pictured) which remains once the cane has been pulverised and the sugar-bearing juice squeezed out of it. World production of cane sugar was 185m tonnes in 2017. That results in a lot of bagasse.
 At the moment, most of this is burned. Often, it fuels local generators that power the mills, so it is not wasted. But Zhu Hongli, a mechanical engineer at Northeastern University in Boston, thinks it can be put to better use. As she and her colleagues describe in  Matter this week, with a bit of tweaking bagasse makes an excellent—and biodegradable—replacement for the plastic used for disposable food containers such as coffee cups.
  Dr Zhu is not the first person to have this idea. But previous attempts tended not to survive contact with liquids. She thought she could overcome that by spiking the sugar cane pulp with another biodegradable material. She knew from previous research that the main reason past efforts fell to pieces when wet is that bagasse is composed of short fibres which are unable to overlap sufficiently to confer resilience on the finished product. She therefore sought to insert a suitably long-fibred substance.
 Bamboo seemed to fit the bill. It grows quickly, degrades readily and has appropriately long fibres. And it worked. When the researchers blended a small amount of bamboo pulp into bagasse, they found that the result had a strong interweaving of short and long fibres. As a bonus, they also discovered that the hot pressing used as part of the process had mobilised some of the lignin in the fibres, and that this stiff, water-repelling material was now acting as an adhesive that bound the fibres together.
 To put their new material through its paces, Dr Zhu and her colleagues first poured hot oil onto it and found that, rather than penetrating the material, as it would have with previous bagasse products, the oil was repelled by their invention. They also found that when they made a cup out of the stuff and filled it with water heated almost to boiling point, the cup remained intact for more than two hours. Though this is not as long as a plastic cup would last (it would survive indefinitely) it is long enough for all practical purposes. Moreover, the new material is twice as strong as the plastic used to make cups, and is definitely biodegradable. When Dr Zhu buried a cup made out of it in the ground, half of it rotted away within two months, and she reckons six months would have seen it gone completely.
 Last, but by no means least, she estimates that cups made from the new material would cost $2,333 a tonne. That is half the $4,750 a tonne cost of biodegradable cups made from polylactic acid (fermented plant starch), and only slightly more than the $2,177 a tonne that it takes to make plastic cups. Overall, then, Dr Zhu argues that bagasse is an obvious choice for making coffee cups, straws, disposable plates, lightweight cutlery and so on. Once used, these could be dumped in landfills with a clear conscience. ■

 This article appeared in the Science & technology section of the print edition under the headline "Would you like sugar cane in that?"
title: 用甘蔗制成的更好的一次性咖啡杯
title_en: Better disposable coffee cups made from Sugar Cane

在一次近2800万张德克萨斯州驾照的黑客攻击中，你的个人信息可能被盗了。一家可以访问车管所记录的保险软件公司表示，它被攻破了。
仅仅是知道我的信息可能被用于欺诈活动就让我感到非常不安，司机amera Guest做出了反应。
近2800万名在2019年2月前拿到驾照的德克萨斯州司机的驾照号码、姓名、出生日期、地址和车辆登记信息被盗。
身份窃贼可以利用这些信息做很多事情。身份盗窃资源中心的詹姆斯·李(James Lee)表示：“他们可以尝试创建一个新账户，当他们登录到现有账户时，他们可以试着证明自己就是你。”
一家名为Vertafort的保险软件公司在一份声明中表示，这些数据无意中存储在一个不安全的存储服务中，该服务在今年3月至8月期间遭到黑客攻击。Vertafort拥有合法访问车管所信息的权限。
Vertafort表示，该公司自8月中旬以来就知道了这起泄密事件。所以我们问为什么他们刚刚宣布了这一消息。
该公司表示，他们向德克萨斯州总检察长办公室、德克萨斯州机动车管理局和德克萨斯州公共安全部报告了此事，并写道，应执法部门的要求，Vertafort的通知被推迟了。
Vertafort说，他们聘请了第三方公司进行调查，但到目前为止没有发现信息被滥用。但是身份盗窃资源中心的詹姆斯·李说，要等一等。
这是他们可能会等待使用的东西。所以你并没有因为一年过去了、两年过去了、三年过去了而走出困境，李解释道。
要非常勤奋地查看信用卡对账单和类似的东西。然后最终考虑升级一些密码。李开复说：“我们今天给人们的建议是，不要有密码，要有密码短语。”
德克萨斯州机动车管理局就这起泄密事件发表了以下声明：信息泄露是由Vertafort造成的。德克萨斯州机动车管理局(Texas Department Of Motor Vehicles)没有遭到黑客攻击，也不是此次入侵的原因。德克萨斯州机动车管理局非常重视保护消费者信息。该部门根据现有的法律法规和行业原则对数据进行分类和保护，并根据国家和机构的数据保留和数据清理政策保留和销毁所有数据。
出于《交通法规》第730章和《联邦司机隐私保护法》(Federal Drivacy Privacy Protection Act)的规定，交通部只允许外部使用信息。这些法律允许，有时还要求向被授权方发布机动车辆记录。
司机可以通过拨打888-479-3560联系Vertafort，了解他们的信息是否被黑客入侵。*Vertafort表示，它将通过Kroll向受影响的人提供一年的免费信用监测，这将有助于解决你的信用报告受到的任何损害。你可以在这里报名参加监控。
身份盗窃资源中心在其网站上提供了更多处理身份盗窃的信息和资源。
note_en:
Your personal information may have been stolen in a hack of nearly 28 million Texas driver's licenses. An insurance software company with access to DMV records says it was breached.
  "Just to know my information could be used for fraudulent activity makes me feel very uneasy," reacted driver Amera Guest.
       Driver's license numbers, names, birthdates, addresses, and vehicle registration information were stolen for nearly 28 million Texas drivers who received a license before February of 2019.
     "There's a lot an identity thief can do with this information.  They can try to create a new account and they can try to prove they are you when they're logging in to an existing account," said James Lee with the Identity Theft Resource Center.
 An insurance software company called Vertafore, which has legal access to that DMV information, said in a statement the data was inadvertently stored in an unsecured storage service that was hacked between March and August of this year.
    Vertafore says the company has known about the breach since mid-August.  So we asked why they're just now announcing it.
  The company says they reported it to the Texas Office of the Attorney General, the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles, and the Texas Department of Public Safety and wrote, "Vertafore’s notice was delayed at law enforcement’s request."
 Vertafore says they hired a third-party firm to investigate but identified no misuse of the information so far. But James Lee with the Identity Theft Resource Center says give it time.
 "It's something they may wait to use.  So you're not out of the woods just because a year has passed, two years have passed, three years have passed," Lee explained.
  "Be very diligent about watching credit card statements and things of that nature.  And ultimately consider upgrading some passwords.  The advice we give people today is don't have a password, have a passphrase," said Lee.
   The Texas DMV issued this statement regarding the breach:  "The breach of information was caused by Vertafore. The Texas Department of Motor Vehicles was not hacked and was not the cause of the breach. The Texas Department of Motor Vehicles takes protecting consumer information very seriously. The department classifies and protects data based on existing statutory regulations and industry principles, and retains and destroys all data in accordance with state and agency data retention and data sanitization policies.
 "The department only allows outside use of information for reasons found in Transportation Code Chapter 730 and the Federal Drivers Privacy Protection Act. These laws permit, and at times require, the release of motor vehicle records to authorized parties."
 Drivers can contact Vertafore to see if their information was hacked by calling 888-479-3560.  Vertafore says it is offering those impacted one year of free credit monitoring through Kroll, which will help resolve any damage to your credit report.  You can sign up for monitoring  here.
  The Identity Theft Resource Center offers more information and resources to deal with identity theft on  their website.
title: 近2800万持有执照的德克萨斯州司机遭遇数据泄露
title_en: Nearly 28M licensed Texas drivers hit by data breach
